metadata.procedure_phase: Stage 2 CLOSED at commit 947cbd5 (Item 2.10.1 narrative corrections, parent 89213ee STATUS append, parent-of-parent 3d1ac35 single Stage-2 commit). EA-6 has retired correctly. Stage 3 (Copy tools per docs/platform_separation/04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml) NOT YET SPAWNED. Guide-#7's first substantive task is to author the Stage 3 EA initialization pointer per the established pattern (Guide-#5 wrote EA-4 + EA-5 init pointers; Guide-#6 wrote the EA-6 init pointer + 11 Guide-#6 disposition files spanning Item 2.6+). The 5 mid-stage handoff XMLs (v1-v5) at ea_outputs/ + 11 Guide-#6 dispositions at temp_for_responses/ + 9 Guide-#5 dispositions + the v4 quality review + STATUS.md (now 246 lines through Stage 2 close) form the audit chain Guide-#7 inherits.
metadata.branch_at_handoff_blarai: chore/platform-extraction @ 947cbd5 (Stage 2 close; 3 docs-only commits ahead of 3d1ac35 substantive Stage-2 commit)
metadata.branch_at_handoff_devplatform: main @ eaa005f (UNCHANGED throughout Stage 2; Stage 2 was BlarAI-side only per stage XML scope; not re-verified this instance)
metadata.status_md_last_commit: 947cbd5 (Stage 2 closure footnote narrative corrections; STATUS.md is now 246 lines including 12-item Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section + A11 anomaly + Stage Ledger row Stage 2 PENDING → COMPLETE + Current State table updated for Stage 2 close)
metadata.fleet_pause_state: paused with original reason "platform separation v2 in progress" + last_updated_by="copilot_agent" + last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved verbatim through 2.5+ days of multi-EA procedure (Stage 1.4 close 2026-04-24 → Stage 2.10.1 close 2026-04-26). NEVER state.resume_fleet was called across the entire Stage 2 procedure.
metadata.worktree_state: clean (zero entries; git status --porcelain returns empty). All 33-entry pre-2.9 working-tree state was folded into 3d1ac35 single Stage-2 commit; subsequent 89213ee + 947cbd5 are docs-only commits. No uncommitted work at this handoff.
metadata.pytest_baseline: 351 passed (pre-Stage-2 282 + V matrix V4-V7 NET-ADD +69 = 351). Held throughout 2.10.1 docstring/narrative work; runtime-no-op for those edits.
metadata.stash_zero: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- byte-identical timestamp held since v3 emission; 11-cycle preservation through v3 → v4 → Guide-5/6 → v5 → 2.7 → 2.7.v2 → docstring/2.8 → 2.9 → 2.10 → 2.10.1. NEVER touched. The cleanest single-axis discipline win in the procedure.
metadata.trigger_reason: la_request "emit handoff" + stage_boundary_recommendation per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml <trigger name="stage_boundary_recommendation"> (HIGH-risk Stage 2 close; "successor benefits from a clean slate at high-risk boundaries"). Guide-#6 proactively recommended handoff at Stage 2 close + LA approved with "emit handoff" signal; Option B (handoff to Guide-#7) selected over Option A (continue with Guide-#6).

guide_handoff_brief.active_conversational_topic:
Stage 2 has just CLOSED at all 4 axes (structural + narrative + audit-chain + invariant) per the final Guide-#6 verdict file (docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_STAGE_2_CLOSED_HANDOFF_RECOMMENDATIONS.xml). EA-6 has retired correctly after emitting the closing handoff to LA in chat (with a final paraphrase-error pattern at the V matrix section, flagged in the Guide-#6 verdict but NOT blocking; chat-only, not committed).
The LA's IMMEDIATE next steps per Option B selection: (1) LA spawns fresh Guide-#7 chat with: GUIDE_PROMPT.xml + STATUS.md + GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml + Variant B chat-init block. (2) Guide-#7 runs Variant B comprehension gate (re-reads all 11-item source-doc set + 11 Guide-#6 disposition files + 9 Guide-#5 disposition files + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs + this handoff brief). (3) Guide-#7 authors the Stage 3 EA initialization pointer per the established pattern (Guide-#5 wrote EA-4 + EA-5 init pointers; Guide-#6 wrote the EA-6 init pointer; Guide-#7 writes the Stage 3 EA init pointer). (4) LA spawns Stage 3 EA with the init pointer + 04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml + standard attachment set.
Guide-#7's IMMEDIATE substantive task (after Variant B gate): author the Stage 3 EA init pointer.

decision.title: Stage 2 closure structure: 3 docs-only commits + 1 substantive commit
decision.what: Stage 2 closed via 4 commits in sequence: - f863a22 (v5 mid-stage handoff XML, EA-5 emitted at item 2.6 → 2.10 transition) - 3d1ac35 (single Stage-2 substantive commit; 69 files / +12955 -74; pyproject.toml in diff per i1-3; commit message verbatim from v5 §work_remaining §2.9 commit_template with X=351 / pre 282 / +69 fill-ins) - 89213ee (Stage 2 close STATUS append; +59/-5 STATUS.md changes; Guide-#6 Item-2.9 verdict XML archived per artifact-management guidance) - 947cbd5 (Stage 2 closure footnote narrative corrections; 5 N-corrections per Guide-#6 authorization; STATUS.md narratives now match verbatim disposition record) Total ack chain depth: 41 v4-era + 4 g6-ea5 + 10 g6-ea6 = 55 acks all SATISFIED at appropriate Item closures.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#7 inherits this 4-commit chain. The single Stage-2 substantive commit (3d1ac35) is the canonical reference for "what Stage 2 did" -- 69 files including chokepoint helpers, ProjectContext resolver, per-project allowlist, --project-root/--project-id flags on 13 Python entrypoints, -BlarAIRoot env-fallback on 6 PS1 helpers, 4 V matrix tests, MCP server D4 DIVERGE retention, D2-completion 6-caller closure. Stage 3 (Copy tools) operates on this baseline.

decision.title: V4 vikunja-process drift Option B AUTHORIZED (rule 5b(a) STATUS-staleness)
decision.what: At Item 2.7 V1-V7 closure assertion run, V4 returned 1 (vikunja-v2.3.0-windows-4.0-amd64.exe PID 18304 running on host) where V1-V7 closure assertion expected 0. Guide-#6 dispositioned per rule 5b(a) STATUS-staleness (NOT procedure breakage per 5b(b)): Vikunja-running on host preserves LA's parallel-workflow productivity (Guide/Co-Lead chats + manual webUI ticket work); Stage 2 XML does NOT require Vikunja stopped (Stage 2 is pure code refactor; no DB access); Stage 4.1 preflight has explicit fail-fast guard (`if ($running) { throw 'Vikunja is still running. Stop it before migrating the DB.' }`) for V4=0 restoration ahead of cutover.
decision.successor_instruction: Vikunja IS running on host throughout Stage 3. Stage 3 is pure copy-tools work (no Vikunja DB access). NO action required at Stage 3 entry. Stage 4 EA's preflight (or LA's panic-card procedure) handles V4=0 restoration before cutover. STATUS.md "Vikunja server" line currently reads "Running on host (per g6-ea6_n8 Option B disposition...; will be stopped at Stage 4.0/4.1 preflight per Stage 4 XML guard, restoring the Stage-0.4 invariant ahead of the destructive cutover block)."

decision.title: D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED retained; Stage 5+ class-method routing follow-up
decision.what: EA-5 + EA-6 chose to KEEP all 7 _require_project_id call sites at handler entry in tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py (DIVERGE from Guide-#6's original chokepoint-only B recommendation). Rationale: chokepoint VikunjaClient class is currently a stub (no HTTP delegation); server.py's _api_*+httpx path never traverses VikunjaClient class methods; function-level _require_project_id calls ARE the chokepoint integration, not redundancy on top of it; class-method routing (the "true B" interpretation) would be a multi-deliverable refactor outside Item 2.6 D4 scope. Guide-#6 APPROVED the divergence with substantive 4-point analysis (Guide-#6 review file 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_INTER_DELIVERABLE_REVIEW_EA_5_D2_INCOMPLETE_V5_AUTHORIZED.xml). Forward-applicable until VikunjaClient class HTTP delegation lands at Stage 5+.
decision.successor_instruction: Stage 3 EA does NOT re-open D4. The D4 decision (chokepoint-function-as-integration via _require_project_id imports) holds through Stage 4 cutover. At Stage 5 or later cleanup, when full chokepoint HTTP delegation is wired, the MCP-bus components (server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py) become candidates for class-method routing migration. Stage 6.7.5-pattern DevPlatform-Meta hardening ticket suggested.

decision.title: D2-completion Option A (HOIST cfg above LiveVikunjaClient constructor)
decision.what: EA-5 D2 PARTIAL (made load_config conditional in legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py.__init__) left the w15 hazard active because the 6 zero-arg production callers still triggered the conditional fallback. Guide-#6 dispositioned Option A (hoist cfg above constructor in the 3 divergent files daily_digest + weekly_summary + run_live; apply AFTER pattern `base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]` uniformly across all 6 callers; preserve assignee variable name 3-3 split: 3 client + 3 live). Verified post-D2-completion: zero production zero-arg LiveVikunjaClient() invocations; ack g5-4 transitions FORWARD-ACTIVE → SATISFIED.
decision.successor_instruction: Stage 3 inherits the post-D2-completion baseline. The legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py:40 conditional fallback remains as defensive safety net for ad-hoc/test usage but is unreachable from production. Full fold of LiveVikunjaClient (deletion of the legacy class entirely) is deferred to Stage 5 cleanup.

decision.title: F-D1-1 + F-D1-2 NO-ACTION dispositions at D1 grep
decision.what: Item 2.6 D1 extended-grep verification net (12 patterns) surfaced two finding families outside the chokepoint-internal/legacy-shim-internal/test-only triage rule. Both authorized NO-ACTION: F-D1-1 (vikunja_mcp/* MCP-bus 19 hits across server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py) → third chokepoint family parallel to chokepoint VikunjaClient + legacy LiveVikunjaClient; §D.1 enforcement preserved at function-level (D4) + at user/protocol-input level (cli.py + bridge/daemon.py); class-method routing deferred to Stage 5+. F-D1-2 (openvino_contrib_agent 7 hits; urllib + GitHub PAT for GitHub API) → out-of-domain by definition; Stage 5.1 keep_in_blarai list preserves it permanently.
decision.successor_instruction: Stage 3 EA does NOT re-investigate F-D1-1 or F-D1-2. Both are forward-staged. Stage 6.7.5-pattern DevPlatform-Meta hardening ticket suggested for F-D1-1 post-Stage-5 (when VikunjaClient HTTP delegation lands). F-D1-2 is openvino_contrib_agent's own concern, not a platform-extraction concern.

decision.title: 5 N-corrections to STATUS.md footnote narratives at 2.10.1 (per dn19)
decision.what: EA-6's initial Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section at 89213ee had 5 narrative errors (LOW-MED severity for footnotes 2 + 7; HIGH severity for 11 + 12.g6-ea6_n7 + 12.g6-ea6_n8). EA-6 framed as rule 5b(a) STATUS-staleness; Guide-#6 OVERRODE per dn19 (in-scope code defect, NOT external state staleness). The N-corrections were applied at 947cbd5 follow-on commit with verbatim text from disposition files. Audit chain in STATUS.md now matches the authoritative records in temp_for_responses/. Recurring paraphrase-introduced-error pattern: caught at v5 count_delta + file 13 docstring + 2.10 footnotes. Each prior cycle was corrected via in-scope follow-on; the closing-handoff chat narrative also has paraphrase errors at V matrix file names but is chat-only (not committed; informational only).
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#7: the EA-6 closing-handoff chat narrative (in this conversation log; LA can scroll up) misnames test_v_matrix_v5 / v6 / v7 files and paraphrases their descriptions inaccurately. The CORRECT names + descriptions are in STATUS.md (post-947cbd5) Stage Ledger row entry + Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section. If Guide-#7 needs to reference the V matrix in any Stage 3 or later artifact, READ STATUS.md or the test files directly; do NOT rely on the EA-6 chat-narrative paraphrases.

decision.title: Artifact management: KEEP temp_for_responses/ + ea_outputs/ in git
decision.what: LA asked at Stage 2 close whether docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ + docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/ should be committed or gitignored. Guide-#6 disposition: KEEP IN GIT. These are the audit chain (Guide-EA disposition exchanges + EA mid-stage handoffs + completion reports + verification evidence). The procedure design explicitly treats them as audit artifacts (dn13: "Do NOT delete or 'clean up' the Guide disposition files in temp_for_responses/. They are committed audit artifacts"). Without them, future maintainers (Stage 6 hardening, post-procedure investigation) have NO record of WHY decisions were made. Stage 6.9 archive plan moves docs/platform_separation/ → docs/archive/platform_separation/ as the long-term home.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#7 authors Stage 3 EA init pointer + future Guide-#7 dispositions in the SAME directory pattern: temp_for_responses/3.X-...xml for Guide responses; ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE3_*.xml for EA mid-stage handoffs. Continues the established naming convention. All committed in git per artifact-management guidance.

decision.title: Closing-handoff timing: BEFORE LA spawns successor, NOT during EA-6 chat
decision.what: EA-6 asked at Item 2.10 close whether to (a) emit closing handoff now or (b) wait for LA acknowledgment. Guide-#6 answer: NEITHER directly -- apply the 5 N-corrections at 2.10.1 FIRST, THEN emit closing handoff. The closing handoff narrative summarizes Stage 2 work; emitting before corrections risks the summary inheriting the same narrative errors. Order: 947cbd5 commit → EA-6 closing handoff in chat → LA "emit handoff" signal → Guide-#6 handoff brief (THIS file) → Guide-#7 spawn → Stage 3 EA init pointer.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#7 inherits the same closing-handoff timing discipline at Stage 3 close (and beyond). The pattern: substantive work → STATUS append → narrative corrections (if surfaced by Guide review) → EA closing handoff → Guide handoff brief → Successor spawn → Next-stage EA init pointer.

open_watchpoints.intro: All watchpoints inherited from prior Guide instances (instance 5→6 brief had 18). Status updates per this Guide-#6 instance:
open_watchpoints.watchpoint: Agents Cadence Monitor LogonType=S4U; Stage 4.7 Unregister + Register requires elevated PowerShell. A3
G-2 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory rewrite at Stage 4.7. Successor must surface to Stage 4 EA. G-2

Vikunja running on host per g6-ea6_n8 Option B disposition. Stage 4.1 has explicit fail-fast guard ('if ($running) { throw "Vikunja is still running. Stop it before migrating the DB." }'). LA's panic-card procedure (per ROLLBACK_NOVICE_GUIDE.md §2) handles V4=0 restoration ahead of cutover. NO action at Stage 3 entry. g6-ea6_n8 / V4-closure-drift

F-D1-1 MCP-bus class-method routing migration: when VikunjaClient HTTP delegation lands at Stage 5+ cleanup (or via separate domain ticket), the MCP-bus components (server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py) become candidates for class-method routing migration. Stage 6.7.5-pattern DevPlatform-Meta hardening ticket suggested. NOT a Stage 3 concern. g6-ea6_n7 / F-D1-1 / D4-DIVERGE-forward
blarai_auxiliary_projects 4 / 7 / 9 investigation at Stage 6 (per Anomaly A10 + Stage 6 hardening directive).

A11 alias-vs-rename optional cleanup (rename --blarai-root → --project-root with deprecation warning). Documented as anomaly per g5-10. Stage 6 hardening ticket if naming hygiene benefit outweighs migration cost.

watchpoint.recheck_at: before any git stash pop / forever

open_watchpoints.watchpoint: Stale wake_launcher auto-stash at stash@{0} (timestamp sdo 20260424_013002). DO NOT POP through any remaining stage. 11-cycle byte-identity preservation now; will become 12-cycle when Stage 3 EA emits its first artifact. The cleanest single-axis discipline win in the procedure.
Worktree precondition (`git worktree list` empty) re-verify at Stage 4.0.5 ahead of destructive cutover.

No merge to chore/platform-extraction → main until LA explicitly approves per R10. Merge gate at Stage 5.10 with explicit Lead Architect approval. The chore/platform-extraction branch is preserved across Stages 3-5 work; merge happens AFTER Stage 5 cleanup completes.

watchpoint.recheck_at: every EA report at every cycle

open_watchpoints.watchpoint: EA-6's paraphrase-introduced-error pattern. EA-6 was structurally sharp throughout but had recurring LOW-MED severity narrative errors at v5 count_delta misattribution + file 13 "8 vs 7" docstring drift + 2.10 footnotes 5-error inventory + closing-handoff chat narrative V5/V6/V7 misnaming. Each prior cycle was corrected via in-scope follow-on commits. Mature response: independent disk verification on every EA report; never trust paraphrased narratives without checking the disposition files. Future EAs may exhibit similar pattern; Guide-#7 should maintain the discipline.

Single-commit discipline (mid-stage handoff commits are docs-only). Stage 3 EA's only valid commits before Item 3.X final commit are docs-only mid-stage handoff XMLs at context-exhaustion threat (per i1-4 inheritance). The Stage 3 final commit folds all refactor + verification work. NO partial- commit refactor; NO emergency single-commit attempts.

guide_handoff_brief.anomalies_not_yet_in_status: STATUS.md is fully updated through 947cbd5. No anomalies pending logging. A11 (alias-vs-rename) is recorded; the 12-item Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section captures all dispositions. Guide-#7 inherits a clean STATUS state. None.

la_profile.intro: All 18 traits from instance 5→6 brief carry forward. Re-confirmed during instance 6 work. One new observation as p19.
la_profile.trait: Non-dev "vibe coder". Re-confirmed throughout Stage 2 procedure.
Prefers XML-formatted Guide-to-EA directives via temp_for_responses/.
**"Mature not minimal" motto.** STRONGLY re-confirmed. The discipline is the procedural backbone.
Forensic analysis discipline; appreciates independent disk verification on EA reports.
Paste-attachment hazards. Not exercised this instance.
IDE focus reminders are context only (e.g., system-reminder noting LA opened 07_STAGE6_HARDENING.xml in IDE during Stage 2 close).
One numbered list per response block. Maintained throughout.
Welcomes proactive handoff recommendations at boundaries. LA accepted Option B handoff at Stage 2 close per stage_boundary_recommendation.
Spell out acronyms on first use.
Guided expertise vs menu-of-options. LA appreciated tabular comparison + Guide recommendation in disposition cycles.
Per-request file-write grants only. Guide writes to temp_for_responses/ are pre-authorized per the GUIDE_PROMPT v2.1 carve-out.
Two-file pattern (XML directive + Markdown brief). Single-file XML pattern was sufficient throughout instance 6 dispositions.
**"temp_for_responses" folder convention** (lowercase, underscores -- the v2.1 GUIDE_PROMPT update).
LA verifies XML output meticulously. Sharp catches at e.g. the count_delta misattribution at v5 emission.
**"Critical operation" + "mature not minimal" = thoroughness signal.** Re-confirmed; LA explicitly asked "is the work mature not minimal?" at multiple Item closures across Stage 2.
**Strategic context-budget conversations.** LA initiated handoff timing conversation at Stage 2.6 boundary (Option B Guide-5→6); same pattern at Stage 2.10 close (Option B Guide-6→7 chosen here).

**LA accepts Guide overrides on EA recommendations when justified.** Multiple instances during Stage 2: f1 disposition Option (a) override; D2-completion Option A authorization; rule 5b(a) vs dn19 override on docstring fix; rule 5b(a) vs dn19 override on STATUS narrative corrections.

**LA's procedural meta-question discipline at stage transitions.** At Stage 2 close, LA proactively asked "should we have it produce any handoff documents? Doesn't look like it produced anything for you nor for the next EA." This was a sharp procedural meta-question that revealed the 3-handoff-types delineation (EA → LA closing; Guide → Guide brief; Guide → next-EA init pointer). Pattern: LA asks meta-questions at stage transitions; Guide answers with explicit role delineation. The LA is not asking because they don't know -- they're verifying that the Guide knows. Future Guide instances should expect similar verification questions and answer with clear role/scope delineation.

active_risk_ids.intro: Risk IDs that proved active during instance 6 work. Carryforward + status updates + new.
active_risk_ids.risk: Worktree contamination. Re-verified clean at every Stage 2 cycle.
S4U elevation; CRITICAL for Stage 4.7. See w1.
resume_fleet vs unpause_fleet. Function-name discipline held; NEVER state.resume_fleet called across the entire Stage 2 procedure.
Stage 4.7 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory. Watchpoint w2 -- carries to Stage 4.
$BLARAI_PID resolution. ctx.vikunja_project_id == 3 returned PASS at Item 2.8 hard gate.
VIKUNJA_PASS literal. Full closure at post-soak.
Vikunja label-ID drift.
Multi-BlarAI-projects discovery; Stage 6.7.5 finalization.
REST vs MCP for Vikunja access. vikunja_access_protocol block in Stages 2/3/4.
_project_context.py schema mismatch.
$BLARAI_PID preflight regex.
Chokepoint guard accepted 0 + negatives. Closed at v4 + 2 NET-ADD tests.
tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py file 13 §D.1 hardening complete.
12-pattern grep verification at Item 2.6 D1 surfaced 2 NO-ACTION dispositions.
Per dn14 + p16. EA-6 maintained discipline; Guide-#7 should continue.
3d1ac35 single Stage-2 commit folded all working-tree work cleanly.
tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py disposition closed via Option A (HOIST + AFTER pattern in 6 callers).
EA-4 confirmed Appendix A.1 SKIP per direct file read.
Compound labels with reference prefixes acceptable when anchor disambiguates. Maintained throughout.
Structural design decision: chokepoint-function-as-integration retained.
live._request → live.update_task closure complete.
test_async_post_gate.ps1 inverted alias direction. STATUS.md A11 entry recorded.

EA-6 had recurring LOW-MED severity paraphrase-introduced errors at v5 / file 13 / 2.10 footnotes / closing-handoff chat. Each corrected via in-scope follow-on. Future EAs may exhibit similar; Guide-#7 maintains independent-disk-verification discipline. See w11.
V4=1 (vikunja running on host) per Option B; restoration at Stage 4.1 fail-fast guard. See w3.

10 g6-ea6 acks (n1 through n10) all SATISFIED at appropriate Item closures: n1+n5 D2-completion; n2 count_delta acknowledgment; n3 v5 doc defects; n4 v6-handoff-readiness; n6 path-confusion; n7 D1 NO-ACTION; n8 V4 disposition; n9 docstring fix; n10 narrative corrections.

source_doc_revisions.intro: Source-doc revisions during instance 6 lifetime. STATUS.md was updated; all 7 stage XMLs + MASTER_PLAN + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW + RECOVERY + INFRA_DELTA_v2 + ROLLBACK_NOVICE_GUIDE UNCHANGED. 11 Guide-#6 disposition XMLs added to docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/. server.py D4 docstring corrected at 3d1ac35 + 2.10.1 narrative corrections at 947cbd5.

revision.commits: 3d1ac35 (initial Stage 2 STATUS untouched at this commit; folded with Stage 2 substantive work) + 89213ee (Stage 2 close STATUS append: +59/-5; 12-item Stage 2 Closure Footnotes section added; A11 anomaly added; Stage Ledger Stage 2 row PENDING → COMPLETE; Current State table updated for Stage 2 close) + 947cbd5 (5 N-corrections to footnote narratives)
revision.note: STATUS.md is now 246 lines (was 192 pre-Stage-2-close). All Stage 2 dispositions documented in the 12-item footnote set. Append-only discipline preserved; A1-A10 untouched; Recovery Anchors / Vikunja Project IDs / How to Resume sections untouched.

revision.commits: 3d1ac35 (folded all temp_for_responses/ ?? entries) + 89213ee (Guide-#6 Item-2.9 verdict XML archived per artifact-management guidance)
revision.files_added_this_instance: 1. 2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_5_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_ITEM_2.6.xml (EA-5 init pointer with v4 quality review C1-C4 corrections + 33-ack chain restatement) 2. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_ITERATION_1_APPROVED_EA_5.xml (EA-5 iter-1 gate APPROVED) 3. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_INTER_DELIVERABLE_REVIEW_EA_5_D2_INCOMPLETE_V5_AUTHORIZED.xml (D2/D3/D4 review + v5 handoff authorization + D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED) 4. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_V5_EMISSION_VERIFIED_WITH_COUNT_DELTA_CORRECTION.xml (v5 emission verification + 4 v5 doc defects C1-C4) 5. 2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_6_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_ITEM_2.6_D2_COMPLETION.xml (EA-6 init pointer with C1-C4 + D1-D5 corrections + 41-ack chain restatement) 6. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_ITERATION_1_APPROVED_EA_6.xml (EA-6 iter-1 gate APPROVED with variable-name-preservation commendation) 7. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_CFG_RESOLUTION_DIVERGENCE_DISPOSITION_OPTION_A_HOIST.xml (D2-completion Option A authorization) 8. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_D2_COMPLETION_VERIFIED_PROCEED_TO_D1.xml (D2-completion verification) 9. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_D1_NO_ACTION_DISPOSITIONS_AUTHORIZED.xml (F-D1-1 + F-D1-2 NO-ACTION authorization) 10. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_V4_VIKUNJA_PROCESS_DRIFT_OPTION_B_AUTHORIZED.xml (V4 vikunja-process Option B authorization) 11. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_ITEM_2_7_CLOSURE_AFFIRMED_PROCEED_TO_2_7_V2.xml (Item 2.7 closure affirmation) 12. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_ITEM_2_7_V2_VERIFIED_DOCSTRING_FIX_AUTHORIZED.xml (Item 2.7.v2 verification + g6-ea6_n9 docstring fix authorization per dn19) 13. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_DOCSTRING_FIX_AND_2_8_VERIFIED_PROCEED_TO_2_9.xml (docstring fix + 2.8 BLARAI_PID gate verification) 14. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_ITEM_2_9_VERIFIED_ARTIFACT_GUIDANCE_PROCEED_TO_2_10.xml (2.9 commit verification + artifact-management guidance per LA's question) 15. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_ITEM_2_10_VERIFIED_NARRATIVE_CORRECTIONS_AUTHORIZED.xml (2.10 commit verification + 5 N-corrections authorization per dn19) 16. 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_STAGE_2_CLOSED_HANDOFF_RECOMMENDATIONS.xml (Stage 2 closed verification + handoff recommendations per stage_boundary_recommendation)
revision.note: 16 disposition files total this instance (some predate the strict Guide-#6 series; the count includes EA-5 + EA-6 init pointers and the EA-5 iter-1 gate review). Successor Guide-#7 reads ALL of these as part of Variant B comprehension gate (in addition to the 9 Guide-#5 disposition files + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs at ea_outputs/).

revision.commits: 3d1ac35 (Stage 2 substantive commit; the corrected "all 7" docstring text was applied here per g6-ea6_n9 in-source authorization)
revision.note: D4 decision block at lines 13-57 now says "all 7" (was "all 8") and removes the phantom "additional project-scoped handler" reference. Optional project_summary clarification note added per Guide-#6 g6-ea6_n9 suggestion (taken by EA-6).

source_doc_revisions.revision_no_change: No changes to 7 stage XMLs (01_STAGE0_PREFLIGHT through 07_STAGE6_HARDENING), 00_MASTER_PLAN.md, AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md, WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md, RECOVERY.md, VERIFICATION_COMMANDS.md, INFRA_DELTA_v2.md, ROLLBACK_NOVICE_GUIDE.md, GUIDE_PROMPT.xml, GUIDE_USAGE.md, CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, devplatform/projects/registry.yaml, BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml. Stage 2 was strictly tools/-side + STATUS-side work; no procedure-doc revisions needed.

first_action_for_successor.step: After your Variant B comprehension gate is complete, briefly acknowledge the handoff and flag any STATUS drift or git advance since 2026-04-26T07:45:00Z. Compare branch_at_handoff_blarai (chore/platform-extraction @ 947cbd5), status_md_last_commit (947cbd5), worktree_state (clean) against actual repo state. Expected new commits since this handoff: only the LA's commit of GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml itself (this file).

**Read the 16 Guide-#6 disposition files in addition to the Variant B 11-item mandatory reads:** all files at docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ that begin with "2.6-" (Guide-#6 era) plus 2.4-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_4 (EA-4 init pointer; carries v4 era context) plus 2.5-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_V4_QUALITY_REVIEW_DEFECTS_FOUND (v4 quality review). Plus the 8 prior Guide-#5 disposition files (2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_*) for full Item 2.4 + 2.5 + 2.5.v2 audit context. Plus the 5 EA mid-stage handoffs at ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v1..v5.xml.
Total audit-chain reading: 16 (Guide-#6) + 9 (Guide-#5) + 5 (EA mid-stage handoffs) + 4 (EA comprehension gates iter-1/iter-2) + 4 (initialization pointers EA-3/4/5/6) = ~38 files. Significant reading overhead; the Variant B comprehension gate is robust by design. Plan ~30-60 minutes of disciplined reading.

**Draft the Stage 3 EA initialization pointer XML** following the same pattern as 2.6-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_6_STAGE2_RESUME_AT_ITEM_2.6_D2_COMPLETION.xml. Adapt for: - EA instance N (TBD; Stage 3 EA = next EA after EA-6; suggest "Stage 3 EA instance 1" since each stage's EA chain restarts) - Stage 3 entry per docs/platform_separation/04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml - Required reading order: 04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml + STATUS.md (post-947cbd5) + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs + 16 Guide-#6 dispositions + 9 Guide-#5 dispositions + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW + INFRA_DELTA_v2 + RECOVERY + EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2 (V1-V7 source) + 00_MASTER_PLAN - Required first actions: V0a-V0M pickup verification (HEAD = 947cbd5; working tree clean; pytest 351; stash byte-identical 11-cycle going to 12-cycle on first edit; state.json invariants; fleet still paused; Vikunja running per Option B; no merge to main) + iteration-1 comprehension gate emission - Stage 3 work scope per 04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml: copy platform-tool dirs to devplatform; update devplatform pyproject.toml; verify devplatform pytest baseline matches BlarAI; Stage 3 single commit at item 3.X (TBD; check Stage 3 XML for specifics) - Carry-forward acks: 41 v4-era + 4 g6-ea5 (SATISFIED audit-history) + 10 g6-ea6 (SATISFIED audit-history). All 55 acks carry forward as audit chain; new acks emerge per Stage 3 dispositions. - Forward-active items: w1-w12 watchpoints; D4 decision retention; D2-completion preserves; F-D1-1 + F-D1-2 forward to Stage 5+. Naming convention: align to procedure-stage prefix per p14. Suggested: docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.0-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_1_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml

Provide the LA with: (a) the file path of the init pointer; (b) a short chat-paste message for the new Stage 3 EA chat ("Execute the attached..."); (c) the attachment list (analogous to the 27-file EA-6 attachment list, plus the new Stage 3 init pointer + 04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs + the 16 Guide-#6 dispositions + 9 Guide-#5 dispositions).

Wait for the LA to spawn the Stage 3 EA + paste back the Stage 3 EA iteration-1 comprehension gate. Review per the GUIDE_PROMPT.xml ea_comprehension_gate_review procedure. APPROVED authorizes Stage 3 EA to begin Item 3.1. Then ongoing review of Stage 3 work + possible mid-stage handoff if Stage 3 EA hits its own context boundary.

prime_stage_3_init_pointer_watchpoints.point: Stage 3 EA init pointer must restate ALL 55 binding acks VERBATIM with status flags. Do NOT summarize or paraphrase. Stage 3 EA must restate them in iteration-1 gate.

Stage 3 EA init pointer must explicitly forward the EA-6 paraphrase-error pattern as a discipline reminder (per w11). Future EAs may exhibit similar; mature response is independent disk verification + in-scope follow-on commits.
Stage 3 EA init pointer must include the V0 pickup verification block (V0a-V0M+ as appropriate). Stage 3 EA's iteration-1 gate must report V0 results.
Stage 3 EA init pointer must include explicit forward acks for Stage 4 carryforward: w1 S4U elevation; w2 G-2 false-green; w3 V4=1 Stage 4.1 preflight; w9 worktree precondition; w10 no-merge-to-main.
Stage 3 EA init pointer should reference the 12-item Stage 2 Closure Footnotes in STATUS.md as the canonical source for Stage 2 disposition narratives (NOT EA-6's chat-narrative paraphrases per w11).

do_not_list.intro: Carry forward all 20 prohibitions from instance 5→6 brief (dn1-dn20). Add 4 new (dn21-dn24) for instance 6 work products + EA-6 paraphrase-pattern continuation.
do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT re-chase STATUS.md line 11 v2-banner HEAD staleness. Known chicken-and-egg.
Pre-execution invariant past-tense issue resolved at Stage 1 EA close.
Do NOT edit, or allow any Stage 3-5 EA to edit, BlarAI's CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or AGENTS.md. V8 / 6.1.v2 deferred.
Do NOT confuse fleet pause with Vikunja server stop. Different concerns.

prohibition.status_change: ACTIVE -- Stage 3 in-scope; MED risk (Stage 3 is MED-risk per Stage map)

do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT relax acknowledgment-wait for Stage 3 EA. Strict verbatim enforcement.
Do NOT debug a "physform-extraction" typo. Chat-only artifact in a prior EA summary.
Do NOT regenerate or revise Stage 0 / Stage 1 / Stage 2 baseline JSON / exit reports / mid-stage handoffs. Frozen audit artifacts.
Do NOT emit a handoff after only a partial Stage UNLESS LA-requested OR per i1-4 trigger.
Do NOT modify this handoff brief or any predecessor handoff brief in git history. Permanent records.
Do NOT allow any Stage 3-5 EA to run `git stash pop` / `git stash apply` on stash@{0}.
Do NOT delete, modify, or "improve" GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_DIRECTIVE.xml or GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_BRIEF.md. Audit artifacts.

Do NOT assume LA's file-write permission to Guide carries forward across instances or across topics within an instance. Per the GUIDE_PROMPT v2.1 carve-out, temp_for_responses/ writes are pre-authorized for Guide responses; otherwise per-request grants only.
Do NOT delete or "clean up" the Guide disposition files in temp_for_responses/. They are committed audit artifacts (or will be at next stage close).

**Do NOT trust the EA's "I did X" reports without disk-level verification.** EA-6 caught at v5 count_delta + file 13 docstring + 2.10 footnotes 5-error inventory + closing-handoff chat narrative -- recurring paraphrase-pattern. Each prior cycle was corrected via in-scope follow-on. Future EAs may exhibit similar.
**Do NOT accept a partial-commit Stage-N mid-stage handoff.** Mid-stage handoff XMLs (vN+) are docs-only commits. Partial-commit refactor work is REJECT.

**Do NOT silently approve scope expansion.** Default disposition for any audit-surfaced PS1 (e.g., critical_notify) is no-action with Appendix A.1 citation. Scope expansion requires explicit LA approval after a discovered runtime BlarAI path. (Distinct from defect closure in already-in-scope work, which is LA-authorized fix in current item per dn19.)
**Do NOT shortcut analysis to save context window.** The LA explicitly chose "mature not minimal." Depth wins over speed.

**Do NOT confuse the legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py with the new chokepoint tools/_vikunja_client.py.** Path-confusion would cause significant procedural drift -- always cite full paths when discussing either file.

**Do NOT defer in-scope defect closures to a different stage when the fix is trivial.** Distinguish ADDITIVE scope expansion (defer; LA approval) from DEFECT CLOSURE (LA-authorized fix in current item). Used at instance 6: f1 Option (a) override; D2-completion Option A; docstring fix in-source; STATUS narrative corrections.
**Do NOT skip the "mature not minimal" disk-verification step on EA reports that look clean.** EA reports do miss things; independent verification on EVERY EA report; never trust report alone.

**Do NOT spawn Stage 3 EA without first authoring the Stage 3 EA initialization pointer.** Per the established Guide-authors-init-pointer pattern across EA-4/5/6, the Guide instance authors the init pointer BEFORE the EA spawn. Skipping this defeats the bootstrap discipline.

**Do NOT skip the Variant B comprehension gate for Guide-#7 (or any successor).** Read all 11-item document set + 16 Guide-#6 disposition files + 9 Guide-#5 disposition files + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs + 4 EA comprehension gates + 4 initialization pointers = ~38 audit artifacts to internalize. The Variant B gate is robust BY DESIGN; shortcuts here propagate ignorance forward.

**Do NOT modify the GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml file (this file) once it's committed.** It's the audit anchor for instance 6→7 transition. The successor briefing happens within Guide-#7's own work, not by editing this brief.

**Do NOT skip independent disk verification on Stage 3 EA narrative summaries** (and beyond). EA-6's paraphrase-introduced-error pattern is now established; future EAs may exhibit similar. Mature response: verify every EA report against disk; surface paraphrase errors via in-scope follow-on commits; do NOT let paraphrase-induced errors propagate into committed audit-chain narratives.
